title: Chunky Leg Console Table
summary: An entryway console with two solid 2x6 legs at each end and a doubled 1x8 top panel — thick, stout legs are the whole design statement, no tapering or shaping.
description:
This console's entire visual identity is its legs: solid, un-shaped 2x6 stock, cut square and left chunky rather than tapered or turned, standing in deliberate contrast to a slim tabletop. The top and bottom panels are each two 1x8 boards joined edge to edge with pocket holes, then notched at the corners with a jigsaw so the legs sit flush into the panel rather than just butting against its face.

Build both panels identically before touching the legs — measure between the jigsawed cutouts on each panel and cut the 1x3 (top) and 1x2 (bottom) trim pieces to match exactly, since panel width can vary slightly from the plan number once two boards are joined. **Level the tabletop before final leg attachment** — the plan calls this out directly: the legs must be level and the top must be level at the same time, which usually means shimming one leg slightly rather than trusting all four cuts to be perfectly identical.

The bottom shelf goes in last, sitting between the four legs and adding a second surface plus real cross-bracing to the leg structure — without it, four freestanding chunky legs would have nothing tying them together below the tabletop.

tools.note: Notching the corners for the legs.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Two boards joined edge to edge per panel, corners notched with a jigsaw to fit the legs.

steps.title: Build the top and bottom panels
steps.body: Join two 1x8 boards together with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws for each panel. Cut out the corners to match the 2x6 legs with a jigsaw. Build two identical panels.

steps.title: Trim the top panel
steps.body: Measure between the cutouts on the top panel and cut the 1x3 trim pieces to match. Attach to the top panel.

steps.title: Trim the bottom panel
steps.body: Measure between the cutouts on the bottom panel and cut the 1x2 trim pieces to match. Attach to the bottom panel.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the top panel, checking that the legs are level and the tabletop itself is level at the same time, shimming as needed.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf and finish
steps.body: Attach the bottom panel between the legs. Sand and finish the whole table.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Entryway console table with thick solid 2x6 legs
